_comment: CURATED variance notes. Hand-verified explanations of WHY a line moved. The automatic layer only describes magnitude; causes live ONLY here. To add a note: append an object with statement (income|balance|cashflow|ratios), line (exact line/ratio label as shown in its table), period (column label, e.g. 'FY2024' or 'Q1 FY24'), explanation, and source_url. Keep explanations factual and cite a public source.

notes.explanation: As reported under GAAP. A one-time, non-recurring ~$10.2B income-tax charge from the EU State Aid (Ireland) ruling, recorded in fiscal Q4 2024, raised the effective tax rate and cut net income despite higher revenue. This is a discrete tax item, not an operational decline; no adjusted/normalized figures are implied.

As reported under GAAP. Net income fell despite higher revenue mainly because of a one-time, non-recurring ~$10.2B income-tax charge from the EU State Aid (Ireland) ruling recorded in fiscal Q4 2024 — a discrete tax item, not an operational decline. No adjusted/normalized figures are implied.
Revenue fell 2.8% YoY - Apple's first annual decline since 2019 - on a strong US dollar, a ~27% drop in Mac sales, soft China demand, and no new iPad launch that year.
Shareholders' equity fell to ~$50.7B because Apple repurchased $90.2B of stock in FY2022 (its largest buyback year to date). Buybacks reduce equity.
ROE spiked to ~197% because Apple's $90.2B of FY2022 buybacks shrank shareholders' equity to ~$50.7B; a smaller equity base inflates return on equity.
